text:
Once upon a time in the serene town of Willowbrook lived a boy named Oliver. Oliver was ordinary, a typical elementary school student, except he had an extraordinary talent — he could hear thoughts. It wasn’t invasive; it was just that he absorbed the quiet conversations happening all around him.

One bright morning, as Oliver breakfasted alone in his loft reading a storybook aloud to his stuffed animals, his thoughts drifted to Mrs. Simmons. She had a habit of gossiping about the neighbors. That day, she was telling Felix about Mr. Jenkins, across the street. "You know he spends all his money on fancy cars," she snickered. Oliver paused mid-book, curious.

In a gentle moment between morning yawns, he murmured, “There’s something better than cars.” Felix cringed. “What?”

“I wish for a pet hedgehog,” I said softly. Felix stared blankly, confused. “A… pet hedgehog?”

I told him all about hedgeh
